text: Genisys Trace Analysis AII Genisys messages are started with a byte which designates the message type, and are ended by an end-of-message character. AII office-to-field messages are acknow- ledged by the field. A 16-bit CRC is used on messages except FI and FB messages. The CRC is included in FB poll messages if the Use Secure POlls bit is set in the mode control word. Office-to-fieId messages: FA - Acknowledgement - responded t0 by an ack FI FB - POII message - responded tO by an ack FI or an indication F2 FC - ControI message - responded t0 by a checkback message F3 if the Use Checkback bit is set, or else by an ack FI or an indication F2 FD - RecaII message - responded tO by an indication F2 FE - Execute message - responded tO by an ack FI or an indication F2 Field-to-office messages: FI - Acknowledgement - no response expected from the office F2 - lndication - responded tO by an ack FA from the office F3 - ControI Checkback - if control is correct, an execute message FE is sent by the office End-of-messaqe F6 An analysis Of several traces follows. ln the Genisys documentation, groups Of eight bits Of data, which we normally think Of as bytes, are called "words”. Various characters and words Of hexadecimal data bits make up the messages.

This 엔OtOC이 trace record consists Of tWO messages, a poll message from the office and a responding acknowledgement from the field. NOte that the Send characters from the Office tO the field are in green and the Received characters from the field to the office are in cyan (light blue). FB 06 C3 32 F6 FI 06 F6 10/15 08:50:22 P CL: 1 N BESSMR Office POII: CURRY HOL Sta: 6 NORTH BESSEMER FB FB 06 C3 F6 06 C3 32 F6 POII message identifier 32 station identifier 06 hex = 6 decimal 16-bit checksum end-of-message character FieId Acknowledgement: FI 06 F6 FI 06 F6 - Acknowledgement identifier station identifier 06 hex = 6 decimal end-of-message character Each trace ends with an information line which contains date/time stamp, the trace record type, the CodeIine number and name, and the station number and name. 10/15 08:50:22 P CL: 1 N BESSMR - CURRY HOL Sta: 6 NORTH BESSEMER The record type is a single letter designation which appears right before the CL: . ln this case it is 'P', which is a POII. The trace type is designated by the Send type, which is Ack (시, POII (리, RecaII (지, ControI (C), or Execute (티.

This 헨OtOCOI trace record consists Of tWO messages, an acknowledgement from the office and a responding acknowledgement from the field. The field acknowledges office messages, even acking the acks from the office. NOte that the Send characters from the office tO the field are in green and the Received characters from the field to the office are in cyan (light blue). FA 09 82 A6 F6 FI 09 F6 10/15 08:23:50 A CL: 1 N BESSMR Office AcknowIedgement: FA 09 82 A6 F6 CURRY HOL Sta. 9 ONE BLOCK FA 09 82 A6 F6 AcknowIedgement identifier station identifier 09 hex = 9 decimal 16-bit checksum end-of-message character Field Acknowledgement: FI 09 F6 FI 09 F6 - AcknowIedgement identifier station identifier 09 hex = 9 decimal end-of-message character Each trace ends with an information line which contains date/time stamp, the trace record type, the CodeIine number and name, and the station number and name. 10/15 08:23:50 A CL: 1 N BESSMR - CURRY HOL Sta: 9 ONE BLOCK The record type is a single letter designation which appears right before the CL: . ln this case it is '사, which is an Ack. The trace type is designated by the Send type, which is Ack (시, POII (리, Recall (R), Control (C), or Execute (티.

ThiS 한OtOC이 trace record consists Of tWO messages, a control message sent to the field and a control checkback received from the field This is followed by the control bit-state trace which shows the control device names and their state of ON or OFF. Control trace record: FC OF 00 25 01 00 02 00 EO 07 143R-OFF 136RR—OFF 152RR—OFF 150COS-OFF FO 08 A5 F6 F3 149N-ON 138RR—OFF 162RR—OFF 152COS-OFF OF 00 25 01 00 02 00 EO 07 C8 95 F6 143N-ON 163R-ON 150RR—OFF 146COS-OFF 10/14 15:28:18 control: C 149R-OFF 142RR—OFF 164RR—OFF 162COS-OFF Sta: 15 0 163N-OFF 146RR—OFF 142COS-OFF 164COS-OFF CENTRAL 1 N BESSMR - CURRY HOL CL: 00 02 00 EO 07 FO 08 A5 F6 FC FC OF 00 01 02 EO FO F6 OF 00 25 이 25 00 00 07 control message identifier station identifier OF hex = 15 decimal control word 0 and its value control word 1 and its value control word 2 and its value Mode control data 16-bit checksum* 08 A5 end-of-message character control checkback F3 F3 OF 00 01 02 EO C8 F6 OF 00 25 01 00 02 00 EO 07 C8 95 F6 25 00 00 07 95 control checkback identifier station identifier OF hex = 15 decimal control word 0 and its value control word 1 and its value control word 2 and its value Mode control data 16-bit checksum end-of-message character ln Genisys, the controls are sent out only for the control words which need tO change. The word numbering starts with 0. ln this case, the control words numbered 0, 1 and 2 are sent. The control information is sent out as [word number] [value] pairs. [0이 [25] signifies control word 0, with a value Of 25. The bit order Of the data is reversed from what is tabled for the code system database. ln Other words, a data value Of 25 (25 = 0010 0101) is in bit-reversed order.

If this is reversed to 10100100, it then matches up with the device tabling 25= word0, bits 1 -8 1 143N-ON 1 163R-ON 0 143R-OFF 0 1 149N-ON 0 0 149R-OFF 0 163N-OFF (*UNUSED *UNUSED *Note that the unused bits are not shown on the screen. 00= word 1, bits 9-16 0 138RR—OFF 0 0 142RR—OFF 0 0 146RR—OFF 0 0 136RR—OFF 0 150RR—OFF 152RR—OFF 162RR—OFF 164RR—OFF Bear in mind that the bit-states that shOW in the trace dO not contain any space-holders for the unused bits, and that bit 6 is next to bit 9 in the bit-state trace. Even if only one control word is sent, we ShOW the control bit-state trace for the entire station each time SO it is easier tO compare and track all of the bits from one control to the next when examining the trace. Likewise, the field only indicates the field changes, and we may only get one or tWO indication words when we receive an indication. We always show all of the indication bit states in the bit-state trace. When a recall is done, however, the field does send back all of the indications for the station. *NOTE: A quirk Of Genisys can be noted in the 한OtOCOl trace for the control. Whenever a data or checksum byte is sent which starts with F, like a hex FO or F7, it is broken into tWO words. For example, F8 gets sent as FO 08, and FO gets sent as FO 00. This is a method tO ensure that no data appears as the reserved message identifier characters FI, F2, F3, FC, etc. The character FF is undefined and not allowed because noise on the transmission lines Often appears as a hex value of FF.

The EO 07 pair Of bytes in the trace are a mode command/data pair. The EO address word and the data are sent by the office tO set the mode for the field. The field should always follow the office mode. The EO command word is followed by an eight-bit word having the following bit definitions with the low-order bit on the right 7 654 32 1 0: Bit 0 - Database complete Bit 1 - Use check-back controls Bit 2 - Use secure polls Bit 3 - Allow common-control command Bit 4 - Unused Bit 5 - Unused Bit 6 - Unused Bit 7 - Unused The EO indication word has the following meanings: Bit 0 - Database complete Bit 1 - Use check-back controls Bit 2 - Use secure polls - Allow common-control command Bit3 Bit 4 - Unused Bit 5 - Unused Bit 6 - Unused Bit 7 - Unused The database will be incomplete for the field when the field unit initializes. After it receives all control bits from the office, it will have its database complete. The office database is complete when all the indication bit states are known. The CRC uses the common generator polynomial )(^16 + )(^15 + x^12 + 1. If the Use Secure POIIS bit is set, then POIIS sent by the office will contain a 16-bit CRC. If the Use Checkback ControIs bit is not set, then the Execute and Checkback messages are not used or defined. A control message will be immediately implemented by the field and the field will respond with FI or F2. We do not use the Common-controI command, which is a high-level function used in communicating with Genysis field units. We normally set Use Checkback ControIs tO ON, and Use Secure POlls set to ON.

ThiS 한OtOC이 trace record consists Of tWO messages, a control execute message sent tO the field and an indication message received from the field. This is followed by the indication bit-state trace which shows the device names and their states of ON or OFF.
